name: MikroTik LHG XL HP5 4-pack
title: MikroTik LHG XL HP5 4-pack
short_description: Light Head Grid XL HP5, 5GHz, 27dBi, TX Power 28dBm, 1xLAN, 802.11a/n, up to 40km, RouterOS L3, 4-pack
description: MikroTik LHG XL HP5 4-pack description. Light Head Grid XL HP5, 5GHz, 27dBi, TX Power 28dBm, 1xLAN, 802.11a/n, up to 40km, RouterOS L3, 4-pack

detailed_description.heading: Mitigating Interference & Amplifying Capacity
detailed_description.text: The MikroTik LHG series consists of compact and lightweight wireless devices, each featuring a dual polarization grid antenna. These antennas are engineered to transmit and receive signals across both horizontal and vertical polarizations, significantly enhancing signal quality and bandwidth efficiency. The feature is paramount in areas prone to interference or in situations where network capacity needs to be amplified without additional frequency allocation. The series' design, characterized by its grid structure, not only mitigates wind load but also sharpens signal directionality, a critical factor for maintaining robust connections over extensive distances.

detailed_description.heading: Enhanced Transmission Efficiency
detailed_description.text:
The MikroTik LHG XL HP5, offered in a 4-pack, is an advanced model within the Light Head Grid (LHG) series, which is valued for its compact, lightweight design, and notably, its effective dual polarization grid antenna. This sophisticated antenna technology leverages both vertical and horizontal polarizations, enhancing signal transmission and reception efficiency, thereby improving the connection's quality and reliability. The grid's design also plays a pivotal role in diminishing wind impact and sharpening signal focus.
Featuring an integrated antenna design, the LHG XL HP5 minimizes cable losses and is built to withstand wind, ensuring stable operation in various environmental conditions. This model supports the 802.11a/n wireless standards and offers a 5GHz radio rate of 300Mbps, catering to users needing efficient, high-speed wireless connectivity. The antenna's frequency range extends from 5.15 to 5.875 GHz, with a notable gain of 27 dBi and a beamwidth of 6.4 degrees, allowing for precise directional communication over extensive distances.

detailed_description.heading: Extended Operational Range
detailed_description.text:
The LHG XL HP5 features a high power output, enabling it to transmit signals with increased strength. This enhancement not only extends the device's operational range but also ensures signal integrity in challenging environments, mitigating obstacles and minimizing signal degradation. The integration of a larger grid and elevated antenna gain in this model synergizes to capture and concentrate the signal more effectively, ensuring its integrity over longer distances, which is indispensable for creating point-to-point (PtP) links. These PtP links, established between two distinct locations, harness the full potential of the LHG XL HP5's directional capabilities, facilitating data transmission across vast expanses without the need for physical cabling. This attribute renders the device especially valuable in scenarios where installing cables is impractical or prohibitively expensive, such as in remote or rural settings, or for connecting isolated network segments.
As Customer Premises Equipment (CPE), the LHG XL HP5 excels in long-distance applications, leveraging its high power output and significant antenna gain to offer steadfast and reliable connectivity to central networks. This capability is crucial for extending broadband services to areas beyond the reach of traditional infrastructure, highlighting the device's role in bridging the digital divide.

detailed_description.heading: Streamlined Networking Operations
detailed_description.text:
The assembly process for the device is designed to be user-friendly, shipping in a compact, disassembled format. Once received, the device can be assembled swiftly, with an easily accessible grounding point to ensure a safe and stable installation. On the technical side, the device operates on the AR9344 chipset, a well-regarded component in the networking world, known for its reliable performance in wireless communication devices. The chipset houses a 600 MHz single-core processor that, while not pushing the limits of processing power, provides a solid foundation for the device’s operations. This processor is complemented by 64 MB of RAM, which facilitates smooth multitasking and consistent performance across various network tasks. Additionally, the device is equipped with 16 MB of storage memory, sufficient for the operating system and essential networking functions, ensuring a streamlined and focused operational experience.
Despite these robust performance capabilities, the device remains energy-efficient, with a maximum power usage of just 7W. Connectivity is ensured through a 100 Mbps Ethernet port, which offers adequate speed for numerous typical network scenarios.
